pages.full_text:
We had picked up a hitch hiker on the way to the Iona ferry, & she had mentioned the silvery beaches in the west. So after crossing back on the ferry, we went to the N.W. of the Isle of Mull & played on the sand, with worm casts, & Charles & John swam. It makes a change from squabbling.

   We are having to de tick Tonic twice a day with a mangle on.

   Back at the campsite the boys play ping pong & the midges are bad. I shall be sad to leave Mull tomorrow.

__Fri 6th Aug__

   Soon after leaving the campsite there was a traffic jam. We had heard an ambulance over an hour before. They were cutting a woman out of her car. We turned around & went down to a ferry boat. There we found a Forestry Commission track which wound on on. Gates opened, wooden barriers lifted up, a doubtful bridge, but we arrived at the far side
pages.notes: Handwritten page number at top-left is partially cut off at the spine edge; best reading is '16', consistent with '17' visible on the facing right page. Text continues mid-sentence onto the right page. 'de tick' may be 'de-tick' (removing ticks from the dog 'Tonic'). 'mangle' possibly 'mangle' or '[?muzzle]'. Year confirmed as 1999 by cross-reference with A_0270 where 'Sat 31st July' = Saturday 31 July 1999 and 'Monday Aug 2nd' = 2 August 1999; 'Fri 6th Aug' = Friday 6 August 1999 is consistent.

pages.full_text:
[Hand-drawn sketch of a tall building with windows, annotated:] pale blue house
__Tobermory__

other house colours on sea front row
yellow, blue, black, orange, purple,
lemon, darker blue, pink, rust.
V. attractive, all windows outlined in white. Slate rooves,
stone church.

of the jam, where the queue was just
getting through. A ranger was just locking
the gate, but he opened it to let us through.

   Our problems were not over. We
followed an oil tanker lorry. A van behind
was rude when we allowed it to overtake.
We honked when it stopped. Simon drove off v.
fast before he got a black eye through the
window.

   __Tobermory__ is v. pretty. We had elevenses
& shopped. We were worried about the van men,
who had arrived in the town.

   On to __Resipole__ on Loch Sunart. Glorious
sunshine. Even Simon & Eve swam in the
loch when we arrived. A boat launch ramp.
Good facilities. Ring pong. We have camped
pages.notes: Page contains a hand-drawn architectural sketch (not a route map) of a tall building labelled 'pale blue house' and 'Tobermory', with colour notes for other seafront houses. Handwritten page number '17' is visible at top-right corner. The main narrative text (from 'of the jam…' onwards) is in black ink, continuing directly from the left page. The final section starting 'On to Resipole…' is written in blue ink, suggesting a later addition or a change of pen. 'Ring pong' is likely 'Ping pong' (misread or written informally). Text ends mid-sentence — continues on the next spread. 'Resipole' is underlined; this is a campsite on the south shore of Loch Sunart, Highland Scotland.
